member.name: Sir Oliver Dowden
member.fullTitle: Rt Hon Sir Oliver Dowden MP
member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Sir Oliver Dowden is the Conservative MP for Hertsmere , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Drax Group
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether he or any official or special adviser acting on his behalf has had any communication with the Financial Conduct Authority since 1 May 2026 on its investigation into Drax Group plc.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Neither the Secretary of State nor Special advisers have had any communications with the Financial Conduct Authority on this matter. On 18 June, the Financial Conduct Authority provided my departmental officials with a link to the FCA’s publicly available...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Drax Group
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether she will ask the Financial Conduct Authority to set out the timeline of its enquiries into Drax Group plc from August 2024 to the closure of its investigation, including the date on which the decision to tak
writtenQuestions.answerText: The FCA is an independent regulator, and this independence is vital to its role. While the Government is responsible for the overall framework for financial services regulation, it cannot intervene in the FCA's operational decision-making, including its i...

writtenQuestions.heading: Drax Group
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether any Minister or special adviser has had any communication with the Financial Conduct Authority on its investigation into Drax Group plc since August 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The FCA is an independent regulator, and this independence is vital to its role. While the Government is responsible for the overall framework for financial services regulation, it cannot intervene in the FCA's operational decision-making, including its i...

writtenQuestions.heading: Recreation Spaces
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, if he will make an estimate of the number of new parks required by 2033 to maintain present levels of provision per person; and what steps his Department is taking to support the
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not made a formal assessment of the number of additional parks required by 2033 to maintain current provision per head. MHCLG is supporting the creation of green and recreational spaces through the planning system. Planning policies sho...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Sexual Offences: Sentencing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether his Department issues sentences for trial of the facts cases involving sexual offences.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In any case, including those involving alleged sexual offences, where a defendant is found unfit to plead, the ordinary criminal trial does not proceed in the usual way. Instead, the jury determines whether the defendant did the act or made the omission c...

writtenQuestions.heading: Mentally Disordered Offenders: Trials
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what steps are being taken to protect the public from a person if they are found unfit to stand trial but was found to have done the act in a trial of the facts.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In any case, including those involving alleged sexual offences, where a defendant is found unfit to plead, the ordinary criminal trial does not proceed in the usual way. Instead, the jury determines whether the defendant did the act or made the omission c...

writtenQuestions.heading: Mentally Disordered Offenders: Trials
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many people have been found unfit to stand trial in each year since 2015.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In any case, including those involving alleged sexual offences, where a defendant is found unfit to plead, the ordinary criminal trial does not proceed in the usual way. Instead, the jury determines whether the defendant did the act or made the omission c...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sexual Offences: Sentencing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether he plans to review the sentencing guidelines for under-16s year olds found guilty of rape and sexual assault.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Sentencing guidelines are produced by the independent Sentencing Council, established by the Coroners and Justice Act 2009, in fulfilment of its statutory duty to do so.

writtenQuestions.heading: Sexual Offences
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what recent steps she has taken to help the police handle sexual offence cases.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are funding the National Centre for Violence Against Women and Girls (VAWG) and Public Protection to improve the national police response to VAWG. Their work includes supporting forces to implement Operation Soteria, which consists of guidance and tool...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sexual Offences
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what guidelines the police have for handling sexual offence cases.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are funding the National Centre for Violence Against Women and Girls (VAWG) and Public Protection to improve the national police response to VAWG. Their work includes supporting forces to implement Operation Soteria, which consists of guidance and tool...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sexual Offences: Victims
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, if he will take steps to expedite rape and sexual violence cases so that victim do not wait more than six months for their case to be heard in court.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government inherited a justice system in crisis, with a record number of cases awaiting trial in the Crown Court. Victims are facing an unacceptably long wait for justice and without action, the situation will continue to spiral.The Courts and Tribuna...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sexual Offences: Victims

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government inherited a justice system in crisis, with a record number of cases awaiting trial in the Crown Court. Victims are facing an unacceptably long wait for justice and without action, the situation will continue to spiral.The Courts and Tribuna...

writtenQuestions.heading: Solicitors Regulation Authority

writtenQuestions.heading: Solicitors: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what discussions his Department has had with the Solicitors Regulation Authority on an independent assessment process for trainee solicitors.
